alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment at 60% U-235; breakout window remains 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: Iran's uranium enrichment continues at 60% U-235 purity with estimated nuclear breakout time of 1–2 weeks per global_metrics. Multiple headlines including FDD analysis highlight ongoing disputes over in-country uranium dilution under the nascent US-Iran MoU, raising persistent proliferation concerns despite preliminary deal.

alerts.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon killing 3 civilians; Iran warns of deal derailment
alerts.summary: Israeli forces launched fresh airstrikes on southern Beirut despite Trump administration pressure, killing at least 3 civilians per NBC, Al Jazeera, and BBC (incidents feed, 2026-06-18). Iran formally warned that continued Israeli military action could violate the nascent US-Iran ceasefire agreement and derail the deal.

alerts.title: Middle East Spectator reports Strait of Hormuz access now requires Iranian permission
alerts.summary: ME Spectator Telegram post (~50K views, 2026-06-17) alleges US Navy warships will be excluded from Persian Gulf under new maritime management; US naval vessels and Israeli shipping now require explicit Iranian approval to transit Strait of Hormuz. Post indicates US 5th Fleet relocation to Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea bases.

alerts.title: US negative sentiment on Iran deal rises; 'war' rhetoric gains frequency
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows 54% positive vs. 38% negative, but trending terms reveal political fracturing: 'trump' (84 mentions, +20 from prior), 'war' (17 mentions, marked increase), 'vance' (16 mentions). Discourse shift reflects Republican skepticism and JCPOA comparison rhetoric gaining domestic traction, signaling political vulnerability of deal durability.

alerts.title: Iran cyber threat persists despite preliminary deal; water system attack under investigation
alerts.summary: US officials confirm Iran cyber campaign continuing post-MoU per Nextgov/FCW headline in global_metrics cyber news feed. Newsweek reports Iranian attack on US water supply infrastructure under active investigation, indicating persistent offensive cyber operations despite diplomatic progress.

analysis_note: US-Iran nuclear agreement faces dual pressure: Iran's 60% enrichment timeline remains existential (1–2 weeks breakout), Israeli operations in Lebanon threaten deal viability, domestic US political opposition intensifies, and Iran asserts new Strait of Hormuz control while maintaining offensive cyber operations—creating a brittle equilibrium at threat level 9 (Critical/Imminent Escalation).
